sample.title: Can Cuba’s Economy Survive Trump?
sample.description:
Donald Trump’s blockade of Cuba, condemned by international groups, has caused shortages of fuel, food and medicine. Under threat from the US, will Cuba’s authoritarian government bend to his will? And as Havana seeks to rebuild a shattered economy, will it turn away from socialism and toward capitalism?

Read more on Bloomberg.com:

Cuba’s Bold Free-Market Turn Fails to Convince US It’s Ready to Change
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-02/cuba-economic-crisis-trump-unconvinced-by-free-market-reforms?srnd=undefined

Watch more Weekly Docs: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLqq4LnWs3olXOhUOv4O6r-9OdWTQN83se

sample.title: How Warsh's Task Forces Will Reshape The Federal Reserve
sample.description:
Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is floating major revisions to the central bank’s approach to assets, productivity, data, inflation and public communications. To achieve this, Warsh has created 5 "task forces" while soliciting interest from individuals outside of the Fed. This presents an opportunity for a small unelected group to influence the broader U.S. economy and value of U.S. dollars circulating globally. CNBC's Matt Peterson reports.

Credits
Reporter: Matt Peterson
Produced and Edited by: Carlos Waters
Camera by: Charlotte Morabito
Animation: Jason Reginato
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin
Additional Footage: Getty Images

sample.title: How Trump's $1.8B 'lawfare' fund works
sample.description:
A federal judge in Virginia on Friday temporarily blocked the Department of Justice from taking any further action to create or disburse money from its so-called Anti-Weaponization Fund as one of three lawsuits challenging it proceeds.

CNBC's Kevin Breuninger breaks down how the fund works.

sample.title: Why Off-Season Travel Is Disappearing
sample.description:
As summers are growing hotter and crowds are getting bigger, more Americans are choosing to visit international destinations in the early spring and fall. Airlines are betting on more people taking long-haul flights during shoulder seasons and are offering more international flights year-round. CNBC's Leslie Josephs has more.

Chapters:
0:00 Crowds and heat are driving travelers away
1:05 Airlines are offering more international flights
1:44 International flights are more lucrative

Produced, Shot and Edited by: Zach Green
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Reporter: Leslie Josephs
Additional Footage: Getty Images

sample.title: How Asian food is reshaping mainstream grocery stores
sample.description:
As interest in Asian food rises, mainstream grocery stores are adding more Asian groceries to their shelves to work toward being a one-stop shop for all consumers.

CNBC's Laya Neelakandan explains.

sample.title: Who Is Funding Pres. Trump’s Freedom 250 Celebrations?
sample.description:
CNBC's Luke Fountain is at the Great American State Fair on the Washington Mall for celebrations of the nation's 250th Independence Day. Freedom 250, the sponsor of the state fair, has become politicized because the Trump administration has pushed many events through that, rather than America 250, a bipartisan group created by Congress. It’s raised concerns from ethics watchdogs and Democrats.

Reporter Luke Fountain
Produced, Shot and Edited by Carlos Waters
Production Assistant Irit Skulnik
Senior Director of Video Lindsey Jacobson
Additional Footage: Getty Images
Additional Sources: Natural Resources Committee Democrats, America250.org, Public Employees for Environmental Responsibility, National Park Foundation

sample.title: What happens if Sen. McConnell leaves office before his term ends?

sample.title: Has The US-Iran War Restarted? | BBC Newscast
sample.description:
Today, the US and Iran have been exchanging strikes after hostiles were renewed in the Strait of Hormuz overnight.

Over 80 sites in Iran were targeted by the US, prompting the Islamic Revolutionary Guard to target US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait.

Donald Trump, speaking at the NATO summit in Türkiye, says he thinks the US-Iran ceasefire is ‘over’, and threatened to hit Iran 'hard again tonight'.

Jeremy Bowen and Caitríona Perry join Adam to discuss why despite strikes and such strong comments, peace talks will likely continue.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#BBCNews

US President Donald Trump threatens Iran and says truce is over | BBC News
sample.description:
US President Donald Trump threatened to attack Iran "hard" tonight, hours after saying he thinks the US-Iran ceasefire is "over”.
 
Trump earlier said of the Iranian leadership: "I don't want to deal with them anymore, they're scum. They're sick people, they're led by sick people”.
 
In response, Iran's foreign minister says: "We do not answer vulgarity with vulgarity, but with action: fearlessly and with great valour”.
 
Trump's comments follow both countries exchanging attacks.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#IranWar #DonaldTrump #BBCNews
